title: salix aurita var. pedicellata
intro: Salix aurita var. pedicellata, also known as the pedunculate willow, is a variety of the salix aurita plant, which is a type of shrub or small tree native to northern Europe and Asia. The plant's bark and leaves are rich in salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a traditional medicinal herb used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Salix aurita var. pedicellata include reducing pain and inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Salix aurita var. pedicellata can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to soothe sore muscles, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
